purpose: Monitors multiple independent leading indicators simultaneously to detect cycle turns, treating divergence between indicators as itself informative
components: Credit impulse: 2-6 month lead, strong at 2007/2009/2022 turns, currently approaching threshold
Yield curve: 7/7 recession prediction record, requires term premium decomposition, steepening trap risk
Earnings revision breadth: 8/9 episodes with 4-8 month lead, currently at ~45-50% (declining from ~60%)
Credit-equity basis: useful but not the single best indicator, model-dependent via Merton framework
Output gap: explains 60-70% of secular equity return variation at 3-year horizons, currently +0.5-1.5%
current_assessment: All three primary indicators are directionally consistent with late-cycle positioning: credit impulse approaching threshold, ERB declining, yield curve in steepening trap zone. No single indicator is superior — the synthesis recommends monitoring all simultaneously and treating divergence as informative.
limitations: Debate declared primary indicator question a draw — no consensus on which is best
ERB signal degraded by cap-weighted/equal-weighted bifurcation
Yield curve decomposition introduces model dependence (30-50bp ACM/Kim-Wright divergence)
Actionable lead time may be only 1-3 months (inside institutional rebalancing noise)
